name: Itxina Natural Monument Loop Trail
name_original: Axkorrigan, Ojo de Atxular y cueva de Superlegor— bucle trailrunning por el biotopo Protegido de Itxina
description: This difficult 6.5-mile trailrunning loop in Itxina Protected Biotope explores the Ojo de Atxular and Supelegor Cave with 1259 feet of gain.
region_intro: Jogging around Areatza offers diverse natural landscapes within the Arratia valley, protected by the Gorbeia mountain range. The region is characterized by the dramatic karstic formations of the Itxina Massif, the expansive Gorbeia Natural Park, and the gentler terrain of the Wetland of Saldropo. The River Arratia flows through the area, providing scenic riverside paths amidst lush flora. Best jogging routes around Areatza The most popular jogging route is Itxina Natural Monument Loop Trail, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the unique eroded rock formations of the Itxina Massif. Another top favourite among local runners is Gorbea Summit Cross loop from Zeanuri, a difficult 19.9 miles (32.1 km) path. This challenging route ascends towards the Gorbeia Summit, offering extensive mountain views. Local runners also love the Gorbea Summit Cross loop from Itxinako natura monumentua, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through mountain terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Jogging around Areatza is defined by mountain trails, karstic landscapes, and riverside paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to challenging mountain runs. The routes in Areatza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 1200 runners have used komoot to explore Areatza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Areatza?
A: Areatza offers a wide selection of over 45 running routes, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Gorbeia Natural Park, from challenging mountain trails to more relaxed riverside paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes around Areatza?
A: Jogging routes in Areatza feature a mix of terrains. You'll find challenging mountain trails, unique karstic landscapes within the Itxina Massif, and gentler riverside paths along the River Arratia. The region is known for its lush greenery, including pine and cypress trees, providing shaded sections.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Areatza?
A: Yes, Areatza offers routes suitable for beginners and those looking for an easier jog. For instance, the Wetland of Saldropo provides a low-difficulty, 2.2 km route perfect for a leisurely run or warm-up. The area also has paths like Paseo del Karpo and Iturrimorro, accessible from the town center, which are ideal for relaxed jogs amidst natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Areatza?
A: Many of the running routes in Areatza are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A notable example is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058694733/itxina-natural-monument-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itxina Natural Monument Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, which is a 6.5-mile (10.5 km) difficult route exploring the unique eroded rock formations. Another option is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6976746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit Cross loop from Zeanuri&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, a challenging 19.9-mile (32.1 km) path.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Areatza?
A: Areatza's running routes are rich with natural beauty and landmarks. You can encounter the dramatic karstic formations of the Itxina Massif, the serene Wetland of Saldropo, and the picturesque Uguzpe Waterfall. The region is also home to several prominent peaks, such as &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4136536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3221012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saibi Summit Cross&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;. Additionally, there are fascinating caves like &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1563667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abaro Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mairulegorreta&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; nearby.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Areatza offers several difficult and long-distance trails. The &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6976746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit Cross loop from Zeanuri&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; is a demanding 19.9-mile (32.1 km) route with significant elevation gain, ascending towards the Gorbeia Summit. Another option is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32025770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit Cross – Mountain Peak View loop from Altzagorta&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, spanning 21.9 km.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Areatza?
A: The running routes in Areatza are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. Over 1200 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning natural landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the diverse options for different skill levels.

Q: Is Areatza suitable for family-friendly jogging or walks?
A: Yes, Areatza offers options for family-friendly activities. While many routes are challenging, the Wetland of Saldropo provides a gentler landscape with easier routes suitable for leisurely jogs or walks with family. The Paseo del Karpo and Iturrimorro areas, accessible from the town center, also offer tranquil paths amidst natural beauty, ideal for a relaxed family outing.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Areatza?
A: Areatza is an excellent destination for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. The lush greenery, including pine and cypress trees, offers shaded paths ideal for running, especially during warmer months. The Uguzpe Waterfall is particularly picturesque during the rainy season. It's always advisable to check local weather conditions before heading out, especially for mountain routes.

Q: Are there routes that offer views of Gorbeia Mountain?
A: Many routes in Areatza provide stunning views of the majestic Gorbeia Mountain, which is the highest peak in Bizkaia province. Routes like the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10519767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit Cross loop from Itxinako natura monumentua&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6976746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit Cross loop from Zeanuri&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; ascend towards the summit, offering extensive mountain vistas and dramatic backdrops of the Gorbeia Natural Park.

Q: What are the options for parking when accessing Areatza's running trails?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, Areatza serves as a gateway to Gorbeia Natural Park, which typically offers designated parking areas for visitors accessing its trails. It's recommended to research specific trailheads or visitor centers within the park, such as the one in Areatza, for detailed parking information before your visit.

Q: Can I access the running routes in Areatza using public transport?
A: Areatza is located within the Arratia valley and serves as a starting point for many mountain trips into Gorbeia Natural Park. While public transport options directly to every trailhead might be limited, the town of Areatza itself is generally accessible. For specific routes, it's best to check local bus schedules or regional transport services that connect to Areatza or key access points within the Gorbeia Natural Park.
tips: Natural gateway to the interior of the Itxina Massif and to Basque mythology.
Summit in the protected area of Itxina. It is the northernmost peak characterized by the limestone cliffs that surround it.
Spectacular cave with a large entrance and very easy to navigate.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or casual jogging?
faqs.answer: No, this route, despite being categorized as 'jogging' on komoot, is generally not recommended for casual jogging or beginners. It is considered a hard, highly technical hike requiring good physical fitness, sure-footedness, sturdy footwear, and prior alpine experience. The terrain includes significant elevation gain, scrambling sections, and uncomfortable karst pavement (lapiaz).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is challenging and diverse, characterized by a dramatic karst landscape within the Itxina Protected Biotope. You'll encounter a 'labyrinth between mountains full of chasms and caves,' with a 'tortuous territory of sinkholes, dolines, and lapiaces.' The route consists primarily of unpaved, natural, and alpine sections, with rocky, uneven ground that makes it unsuitable for typical jogging.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers several spectacular natural highlights. You will pass by the famous Ojo de Atxular, a natural gateway with panoramic views. The trail also leads to the Axkorrigan Occidental Summit (1095 m), offering breathtaking views over the Itxina Karst. Additionally, you can visit the impressive Supelegor Cave, known for its large entrance and mythical connections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided in the available information. However, trails in the Gorbeia Natural Park often have designated parking areas near popular access points. It is advisable to check local resources or maps for the most convenient and official parking locations before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access the Itxina Protected Biotope?
faqs.answer: The Itxina Protected Biotope is located within the Gorbeia Natural Park. Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the natural park itself. However, for specific activities or larger groups, it's always best to check the official websites of Visit Biscay (visitbiscay.eus) or Basque Country Tourism (basquecountry-tourism.com) for any updated regulations or permit requirements, especially for sensitive areas or guided tours.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to undertake this route?
faqs.answer: Given the technical nature of the terrain, especially the steep and rocky sections, it's best to undertake this route during dry conditions. Wet ground can make the scramble and steep channels particularly hazardous. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures, but always check the weather forecast for the Gorbeia Natural Park before you go.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in natural parks allow dogs, the highly technical and potentially hazardous terrain of this specific route, including scrambling and navigating uncomfortable karst sections, might not be suitable for all dogs. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, physically capable of handling difficult terrain, and kept on a leash as per general park regulations.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and terrain, you should bring sturdy, ankle-supporting hiking boots with good grip, appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions, plenty of water and snacks, a map and compass or GPS device (as straying from the path can be problematic), and a headlamp or flashlight if you plan to explore the Supelegor Cave.

faqs.question: Are there any specific weather conditions to be aware of?
faqs.answer: Yes, particular caution is advised if the ground is wet. The ascent to Axkorrigan involves a steep channel of rock and grass which becomes especially discouraged and dangerous in wet conditions. The karst terrain can also be very slippery when wet. Always check the weather forecast for the Gorbeia Natural Park before starting your journey.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route also passes through sections of the [CIMA BI02] Larreder - Pagomakurre and the Areatza - Pagomakurre trails.

faqs.question: Can I explore the caves mentioned on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route allows for visits to notable caves like Supelegor Cave. Supelegor is celebrated for its impressive, large entrance and accessible interior. A flashlight or headlamp is highly recommended if you plan to explore inside.
